reasoning:
The most recent value for DGS2 as of 2026-01-30 is 3.52%, which represents a local trough in the recent 30-day window (down from a high of 3.61% on Jan 22). The statistical trend analysis provides a primary anchor of 71% probability that the yield will be higher than 3.52% by early February, as the linear trend models a reversion toward 3.60%. Fundamentally, this is supported by the Jan 29 Federal Reserve decision to leave rates unchanged (at 3.50-3.75%) and cite 'somewhat elevated' inflation, meaning a 2-year yield of 3.52% may be slightly over-pricing near-term rate cuts. 

I temper the 71% estimate slightly to 68% for the Feb 8 resolution date due to the direct downward momentum over the last week of January. For the distant Mar 3 resolution date, uncertainty naturally increases in financial markets, so the estimate is drawn closer to a 50% uninformative prior, resulting in 58%.
